title: azalea nudiflora var. canescens
intro: Azalea nudiflora var. canescens, also known as the Hoary Azalea, is a deciduous shrub native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its white or pale pink flowers and grayish-brown hairy leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Azalea nudiflora var. canescens include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to promote relaxation, reduce fever, and soothe digestive issues.
